edges.source: Ameloblast Dysfunction and Defective Enamel Biomineralization
Ameloblast Dysfunction and Defective Enamel Biomineralization
edges.target: Defective Enamel Matrix Processing and Maturation

edges.source: Defective Enamel Matrix Processing and Maturation

nodes.description: AI is fundamentally a disorder of the ameloblast, the ectoderm-derived epithelial cell that builds enamel. Enamel formation is a tightly staged biomineralization process in which ameloblasts first secrete an organic enamel matrix and then drive its mineralization. Mutations in the genes critical for this process disrupt ameloblast function and produce structurally and/or quantitatively abnormal enamel. The specific inherited dental phenotype maps onto the developmental stage at which the mutated gene is critically required.
nodes.id: Ameloblast Dysfunction and Defective Enamel Biomineralization

evidence.snippet: Dental enamel formation is a remarkable example of a biomineralization process.
The type of inherited dental malformations observed in a given kindred relate to when, during odontogenesis, the defective gene is critically expressed.

nodes.description: During the maturation stage, the enamel proteases MMP20 (enamelysin) and KLK4 (kallikrein-4) progressively degrade and remove the enamel matrix, allowing crystallites to grow and the tissue to harden. Biallelic mutations in MMP20 and KLK4 impair matrix processing, leaving residual protein and soft, hypomineralized/hypomature enamel.
nodes.id: Defective Enamel Matrix Processing and Maturation

evidence.snippet: Genes expressed by odontoblasts (COL1A1, COL1A2, and DSPP), and ameloblasts (AMELX, ENAM, MMP20, and KLK4) during the crown formation stage, are associated with dentinogenesis imperfecta, dentin dysplasia, and amelogenesis imperfecta.

nodes.description: During the secretory stage, ameloblasts deposit an enamel matrix rich in amelogenin (AMELX) and enamelin (ENAM) that templates the elongating enamel crystallites. Mutations in AMELX (X-linked) and ENAM (autosomal) impair matrix deposition, reducing enamel thickness and producing the hypoplastic AI phenotype.

evidence.snippet: Mutations in the amelogenin gene (AMELX) cause X-linked amelogenesis imperfecta, while mutations in the enamelin gene (ENAM) cause autosomal-inherited forms of amelogenesis imperfecta.

nodes.description: The proximate consequence of disrupted secretion, processing, or mineralization is enamel that is thin, soft, discolored, and/or rapidly worn. This is the structural phenotype underlying the clinical presentation of AI: hypoplastic, hypomature, or hypocalcified enamel with attendant sensitivity, attrition, and esthetic impairment.

evidence.snippet: The amelogenesis imperfectas (AIs) are a clinically and genetically diverse group of conditions that are caused by mutations in a variety of genes that are critical for normal enamel formation.
